title: rubus corylifolius subsp. oreogeton
intro: Rubus corylifolius subsp. oreogeton, also known as the Japanese bramble or wild raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus corylifolius species, a type of bramble or thorny shrub. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of anthocyanins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus corylifolius subsp. oreogeton include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to have anti-cancer properties. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs in traditional Japanese medicine.
